Endpoints.description: An Endpoints object is returned when a DescribeEndpoints request is made.

CachePeriodInMinutes.description: The TTL for the endpoint, in minutes.

NextToken.description: A token to specify where to start paginating. Provide the next ListBatchLoadTasksRequest.

BatchLoadTasks.description: A list of batch load task details.

TaskId.description: The ID of the batch load task.

DatabaseName.description: Database name for the database into which a batch load task loads data.

TableName.description: Table name for the table into which a batch load task loads data.

CreationTime.description: The time when the Timestream batch load task was created.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The time when the Timestream batch load task was last updated.

NextToken.description: The pagination token. This parameter is returned when the response is truncated.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name that uniquely identifies this database.

TableCount.description: The total number of tables found within a Timestream database.

KmsKeyId.description: The identifier of the KMS key used to encrypt the data stored in the database.

CreationTime.description: The time when the database was created, calculated from the Unix epoch time.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The last time that this database was updated.

NextToken.description: A token to specify where to start paginating. This is the NextToken from a previously truncated response.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name that uniquely identifies this table.

DatabaseName.description: The name of the Timestream database that contains this table.

TableStatus.description:
The current state of the table:

  • DELETING - The table is being deleted.

  • ACTIVE - The table is ready for use.

RetentionProperties.description: The retention duration for the memory store and magnetic store.

MemoryStoreRetentionPeriodInHours.description: The duration for which data must be stored in the memory store.

MagneticStoreRetentionPeriodInDays.description: The duration for which data must be stored in the magnetic store.

CreationTime.description: The time when the Timestream table was created.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The time when the Timestream table was last updated.

MagneticStoreWriteProperties.description: Contains properties to set on the table when enabling magnetic store writes.

EnableMagneticStoreWrites.description: A flag to enable magnetic store writes.

MagneticStoreRejectedDataLocation.description: The location to write error reports for records rejected asynchronously during magnetic store writes.

S3Configuration.description: Configuration of an S3 location to write error reports for records rejected, asynchronously, during magnetic store writes.

BucketName.description: The bucket name of the customer S3 bucket.

ObjectKeyPrefix.description: The object key preview for the customer S3 location.

EncryptionOption.description: The encryption option for the customer S3 location. Options are S3 server-side encryption with an S3 managed key or Amazon Web Services managed key.

KmsKeyId.description: The KMS key ID for the customer S3 location when encrypting with an Amazon Web Services managed key.

CompositePartitionKey.description: A non-empty list of partition keys defining the attributes used to partition the table data. The order of the list determines the partition hierarchy. The name and type of each partition key as well as the partition key order cannot be changed after the table is created. However, the enforcement level of each partition key can be changed.

TaskId.description: The ID of the batch load task.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The last time that this database was updated.

The name and type of each partition key as well as the partition key order cannot be changed after the table is created. However, the enforcement level of each partition key can be changed.

TaskId.description: The ID of the batch load task.

DataSourceConfiguration.description: Configuration details about the data source for a batch load task.

DataSourceS3Configuration.description: Configuration of an S3 location for a file which contains data to load.

BucketName.description: The bucket name of the customer S3 bucket.

CsvConfiguration.description: A delimited data format where the column separator can be a comma and the record separator is a newline character.

ColumnSeparator.description: Column separator can be one of comma (','), pipe ('|), semicolon (';'), tab('/t'), or blank space (' ').

QuoteChar.description: Can be single quote (') or double quote (").

TrimWhiteSpace.description: Specifies to trim leading and trailing white space.

ReportConfiguration.description: Report configuration for a batch load task. This contains details about where error reports are stored.

ReportS3Configuration.description: Configuration of an S3 location to write error reports and events for a batch load.

DataModelConfiguration.description: Data model configuration for a batch load task. This contains details about where a data model for a batch load task is stored.

TimeColumn.description: Source column to be mapped to time.

TimeUnit.description: The granularity of the timestamp unit. It indicates if the time value is in seconds, milliseconds, nanoseconds, or other supported values. Default is MILLISECONDS.

MultiMeasureMappings.description: Source to target mappings for multi-measure records.

CreationTime.description: The time when the Timestream batch load task was created.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The time when the Timestream batch load task was last updated.

Tags.description: The tags currently associated with the Timestream resource.

Key.description: The key of the tag. Tag keys are case sensitive.

Value.description: The value of the tag. Tag values are case-sensitive and can be null.

Database.description: A top-level container for a table. Databases and tables are the fundamental management concepts in Amazon Timestream. All tables in a database are encrypted with the same KMS key.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name that uniquely identifies this database.

TableCount.description: The total number of tables found within a Timestream database.

KmsKeyId.description: The identifier of the KMS key used to encrypt the data stored in the database.

CreationTime.description: The time when the database was created, calculated from the Unix epoch time.

LastUpdatedTime.description: The last time that this database was updated.

RecordsIngested.description: Information on the records ingested by this request.

MemoryStore.description: Count of records ingested into the memory store.

MagneticStore.description: Count of records ingested into the magnetic store.
